calendar.matters:
Petitioner'S Petition For Alternative Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Apr-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDAMUS.
APPEARANCES: LYNN S. CARMAN, ESQUIRE (714.554.0632) APPEARING FOR PETITIONER. KATHLEEN S. MORRIS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY AND JAMES M. EMERY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3987 - MORRIS) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
LATE ISSUED TENTATIVE RULING: The writ is denied. The writ lacks merit in that the record reflects that there is not to be a reduction in services at Laguna Honda but a reconfiguration of the services to include both residential and community based services. Petitioner objects to the reduction in beds but that does not mean it is a reduction in services. Moreover, the Court of Appeals has recognized the right of Respondent to reduce the number of beds in passing the bonds to rebuild Laguna Honda. Monette-Shaw v. San Francisco Board of Supervisors (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1210, 1220- 1224. Finally, Respondent has scheduled a Beilenson Act hearing for April 12, 2008 thereby negating Petitioner's claim of the lack of a hearing on this specific topic. With respect to the Chambers settlement, Respondent has complied with the applicable ordinances for the approval of the settlement. Petitioner has been fully apprised of the settlements at issue and lacks standing to assert a claim.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. COURT WILL ALLOW THE FILING OF THE APRIL 2, 2008 DECLARATION OF SEAN PATRICK MONETTE-SHAW (SUBMITTED THIS DATE) AND THE VERIFICATION IS TO BE FILED WITHIN TWENTY-FOUR HOURS. COURT WILL ALLOW THE FILING OF LYNN S. CARMAN'S LETTER BRIEF DATED APRIL 2, 2008. MR. CARMAN IS TO PLACE A FACE SHEET ON THE LETTER BRIEF SO THAT IT MAY BE FILED. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
